家族.mnemonics: Think of 'kazoku' as 'ka-zone-cook.' Imagine your family cooking together in a cozy kitchen zone.

お兄さん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, knee, son!' Imagine an older brother who always kneeled down to tie your shoelaces.

お姉さん.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, neigh, son!' Imagine an older sister who loves horses and often mimics a horse's neigh.

父.mnemonics: 'Chichi' sounds like 'chee-chee.' Picture your father playing a game of chess ('chee' for chess).

母.mnemonics: Sounds like laughter 'ha-ha.' Remember your mother's laugh.

兄.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, knee.' Imagine your older brother always hitting his knee on the table.

姉.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, neh.' Imagine your older sister pondering and saying 'Ah, neh' when thinking.

妹.mnemonics: Sounds like 'E-moto.' Picture your younger sister riding an e-motorbike.

弟.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, toe-toe.' Imagine your younger brother always stubbing his toe.

兄弟.mnemonics: Think of 'kyo-die.' Picture siblings playing a video game together and saying, 'If you lose (die) in Kyoto...'

男の人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, toko no hito.' Imagine a man saying, 'Oh, I'm just a 'toko' (guy) in this place.'

女の人.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, nonna hito.' Picture a woman saying, 'Oh, I'm nonna (not a) regular person.'

会社.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kai-sha.' Imagine a company on the coast (kai) near the sea.

食堂.mnemonics: Sounds like 'shoku-dough.' Picture a cafeteria where they always bake fresh dough.

髪.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kami.' Imagine your hair feeling like divine (kami in Japanese means god) silk.

口.mnemonics: Sounds like 'coochie.' Remember by thinking of a baby's coochie-coo, often said while touching their mouth.

目.mnemonics: Sounds like 'May.' Think of your eyes always brightening up in the month of May.
